pi_items.source: Task instruction explicitly requests the user to act as a Marine Routing Meteorologist.
Task description and setup scripts mention Vendée Globe and marine routing weather advisory request.
Explicitly defined in setup_task.sh, referenced in instructions, and verified as a standard Panoply testing dataset.
Extracted variable 'slp' correctly corresponds to Sea Level Pressure from the downloaded NCEP dataset.
Output directory explicitly created in setup_task.sh and referenced by export_result.sh.
Target output paths explicitly checked and required by export_result.sh and task instructions.
Python analysis of the NCEP/NCAR slp.mon.ltm.nc dataset shows SLP at 30°S, 20°E in January is ~1011.0 hPa, falling in this range.
Python analysis of the NCEP/NCAR slp.mon.ltm.nc dataset shows SLP at 60°S, 20°E in January is ~985.2 hPa, falling in this range.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis climatology data downloaded from the official NOAA PSL archive. A critical detail verified through direct metadata extraction from slp.mon.ltm.nc is that the target variable 'slp' is natively stored in millibars (equivalent to hPa). The voyage brief contains a conditional trap suggesting that if the data is in Pascals, the agent should divide by 100. Since the data is already in millibars, applying this division will cause the agent to incorrectly report unphysical values (~10.11 hPa and ~9.85 hPa instead of the valid ~1011.0 hPa and ~985.2 hPa).
